items.title: Training vs unseen data
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  trainData["training set D_train"]
  fitPhase["fit: learn theta"]
  model["model f_theta"]
  unseen["unseen x (and y for eval)"]
  predictPhase["predict on held-out data"]
  genMetric["generalization estimate"]
  trainData --> fitPhase --> model
  unseen --> predictPhase
  model --> predictPhase --> genMetric
items.caption: Generalization is measured on data the model did not train on — predict with frozen θ, score against held-out labels.

items.title: Good vs poor generalization
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  goodLabel["good generalization"]
  lowTrain["low train error"]
  lowTest["low test error"]
  poorLabel["poor generalization (overfitting)"]
  vLowTrain["very low train error"]
  highTest["high test error"]
  goodLabel --> lowTrain --> lowTest
  poorLabel --> vLowTrain --> highTest
items.caption: Overfitting shows a large generalization gap: excellent training fit, weak performance on unseen data.

items.title: Generalization gap
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  trainErr["train error"]
  testErr["test error"]
  gapNode["gap = test - train"]
  trainErr --> gapNode
  testErr --> gapNode
items.caption: The gap between test and training error summarizes how much performance drops on unseen examples.

items.title: Estimating generalization before deployment
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  allData["labeled dataset"]
  holdout["hold-out test OR cross-validation folds"]
  trainSplit["train split"]
  evalSplit["eval split (never fit)"]
  estimate["generalization estimate"]
  allData --> holdout
  holdout --> trainSplit
  holdout --> evalSplit
  trainSplit --> estimate
  evalSplit --> estimate
items.caption: Hold-out sets and cross-validation estimate how the model will behave on unseen data before live inference.
